title: Orphaned-dep reclaim: ownerless weight files have no UI path to delete them (size claim needs re-measuring)
description:
CONFIRMED ON DISK 2026-07-19 (user report + verified): all shared LTX-2.3 deps are present (gemma_3_12B fp4, video+audio VAE, text projection, spatial upscaler, 3 LoRAs = 19GB by du) while BOTH exclusive transformers are absent. Nothing owns them. This is the fossil of MPI-258 B1: the pre-258 per-dep guard kept these files on an uninstall 'because the sibling has them on disk' while the sibling was never installed. The 258/276 fullyInstalled fix stopped NEW stranding but never reclaimed existing orphans, and the MPI-310 exclusive-evidence rule (48b76ace) correctly declines to protect them — but a not-installed card shows INSTALL, never Uninstall, so there is NO UI path to free the 19GB. The completeness bars (LTX High 33% / Balanced 45%) are honest and are how the user spotted it.

SHAPE OF FIX (decide at pickup): likely extend the existing Cleanup surface (MPI-227 preview-assets GC precedent) with an orphaned-weights sweep: a dep file on disk that (a) belongs to no whole-model-installed model under the MPI-310 exclusive-evidence rule, (b) is not universal, (c) is not app/plugin-required (union _appRequiredDepIds + _pluginRequiredDepIds), (d) has no in-flight job -> offer reclaim with size. MUST route the decision through the SAME protection primitives in routes/downloadManager.js (_localSharedDepsMap / _multiModelDepIds) — do NOT write a second parallel notion of 'orphan' (engine-split rule: remote twin question exists too, volume orphans on the Pod).

DO NOT auto-delete. User-facing, opt-in, per-file sizes shown. Manual workaround meanwhile: the 8 files are individually deletable from G:/CubricModels (list in du output in MPI-310 events / this card's origin).

events.note: Split from MPI-310 shared-dep work. User spotted partial bars on both LTX cards with LTX never currently installed; disk check confirmed 19GB of ownerless shared deps (both transformers absent) — the literal ~19GB from MPI-258 B1, stranded by the pre-258 circular guard and never reclaimed by any later fix.

Reclaimed on user go-ahead 2026-07-19. Read-only scan (inverting _localSharedDepsMap + _multiModelDepIds + universal/app/plugin guards) found exactly 8 ownerless LTX-2.3 deps = 18.62GB; user approved; deleted from G:/CubricModels + removed the emptied loras/ltx-2.3/ dirs. Free space 56G->75G (+19GB). Re-scan returns 0 orphans. NO code shipped and NO cleanup UI built: the pre-258 circular guard that stranded these is gone and MPI-310 exclusive-evidence closed the opposite cycle, so this was a one-time fossil, not a live leak. Card closes as a one-off reclaim, not a feature.

PREMISE CORRECTED — the '19GB on disk' figure in this card's description is UNSOUND and must not be trusted. It came from a `du` run against a hardcoded 8-filename list with 2>/dev/null, which silences the 'No such file' errors that would have revealed files were already absent; the sum reported whatever subset still existed, not an inventory. Re-checked 2026-07-19 ~20:00: G:/CubricModels holds NO ltx/gemma weights at all (only a stale .cache/huggingface .metadata stub), and G:/CubricModels/vae + loras have dir mtime 2026-07-19T18:29 with the loras/ltx-2.3 subfolder gone. So the LTX weights were deleted at ~18:29, BEFORE the 18:45 measurement. APP EXONERATED for this deletion: app.log records only three uninstalls — plugin:image-describer at 09:46:34 and 16:37:26 (both pre-fix, both 'removed 1, kept 0 shared', the original MPI-310 bug) and TWO post-fix runs at 17:39:07 + 17:39:28 ('removed 0, kept 1 shared' — the guard correctly keeping the encoder). No uninstall ever targeted LTX deps. The log ends 17:40:55 (session restarted), so 18:29 is outside its coverage and the actual deleting agent is UNKNOWN — do not assume, re-derive on pickup. WHAT SURVIVES from this card: the STRUCTURAL gap is still real and still unfixed — a not-installed model card shows INSTALL, never Uninstall, so ANY ownerless dep files have no UI reclaim path. Re-measure honestly before sizing the work (never pipe a file-existence check to /dev/null; list the directory and read what is actually there).
